sessions.intent: I want to understand extension part

records.explanation: The added console.log statement provides immediate feedback that the Nupjuk extension is active on KLMS, indicating its expected operational context to the user.

The code addition enhances debugging and visibility by logging the parsed KLMS data in clearer formats, including a formatted JSON string and a table of assignments, which aids in examining the data structure and content effectively.

The `extractProfileFromKLMS` function is now being introduced to extract a user's display name and student ID from the `.multi-users` element on a page, which wasn't previously handled. It checks for the element's existence, extracts and parses its text content, and logs warnings if parsing fails.

The addition of `const profile = extractProfileFromKLMS();` integrates profile information extraction from the KLMS into the existing data parsing structure, enhancing the extension with user-specific data which is crucial for personalizing or targeting the data handling.
The `parseSemester` function was introduced to extract and combine selected year and semester information, ensuring that semester details are included when compiling parsed data from the KLMS page.
The new line defines 'semester' by calling 'parseSemester()', extending data collection to include semester information when syncing and ingesting KLMS data.

The new function extractKlmsCourseId extracts the 'id' parameter from a URL's query string, ensuring each course URL is correctly associated with its 'klmsCourseId', thus allowing precise course identification and linking.

The new `parseCoursesFromPage` function extracts course data from the page text, identifying course codes, names, and associated elements by parsing line-by-line and matching course links to augment extracted courses with IDs and URLs, enhancing the course data context with link information.

The addition of `const courses = parseCoursesFromPage();` extracts course information from the KLMS page, complementing the existing functions that extract user profiles and semester details to provide a comprehensive data set for syncing.

The `parseAssignments` function is newly added to extract assignments from KLMS by selecting '.list-box.assign' elements, parsing links for due date, title, and course name, and returning structured assignment data with a status indicator for synchronization.

The addition of 'parsedData' aggregates various pieces of information (profile, semester, courses, assignments) extracted from the page, including new entries like page URL and title, creating a centralized data structure for easier access and manipulation.

The code introduces three new POST requests to ingest profile, course, and assignment data to a server, improving data integration by transferring parsed KLMS data to the specified endpoints if certain conditions are met, like the presence of profile data or having courses and assignments in the parsed data.
The addition of 'const token' introduces an authorization token, which is used in HTTP requests to authenticate the user's identity when posting parsed KLMS data to external services.
The new manifest file defines the Nupjuk Campus Sync extension to parse data from KLMS pages by injecting 'content.js' on relevant URLs, which allows interaction with specific web content.

You are explaining code changes to a developer.
Prioritize the user intent when deciding which aspects of this local change to emphasize.
Focus on how the role/behavior of the enclosing function or block changed.
Write the explanation in English. Keep code identifiers exactly as they appear.
Explain why the new code is needed relative to the original behavior. If there is addition which is similar to surrounding code, for example, introducing a new branch or conditional, then compare the meaning of the existing code (branch/path) with the new one.
New definitions in this change: none.
If this change introduces or starts using a new function, explain that function?? role in the change.
Use 1-2 concise sentences. Plain text only; no markdown.
If something is unclear, state that explicitly.
